title: Bridge Essay: Biophilia as Evolutionary Adaptation: An Onto- and Phylogenetic Framework for Biophilic Design.
concept: Biophilia, nature connectedness, neurobiophilia, environmental neuroscience.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ecology/Animism** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Ecology/Animism — biophilia, nature connectedness, environmental neuroscience, neurobiophilia.
**Phase context:** Biophilia, nature connectedness, neurobiophilia, environmental neuroscience.

**Biophilia as Evolutionary Adaptation: An Onto- and Phylogenetic Framework for Biophilic Design.** — Front Psychol (2021). DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2021.700709. PMID: 34367025. Biophilia is a human personality trait described initially by Erich Fromm and later by E.O. Wilson, both of whom agree that biophilia has a biological basis and that it is fundamental to develop harmonious relationships between humans and the biosphere. This review aims at establishing a definition of biophilia as an evolutionary process.
